title: Vector Artefacts Cutover — move work-items + artefacts from mmff_vector to a dedicated vector_artefacts database
scope: Migrate the work-items / artefacts read+write path off the legacy mmff_vector.obj_* tables and onto a new dedicated Postgres database vector_artefacts with a unified artefacts table. Five-phase rollout: Phase 1 stand up vector_artefacts schema and dev fixture (migrations 010–014, 15-artefact seed, deferred-work register); Phase 2 build the backend/internal/workitemsv2 Go package (dual-pool service against vector_artefacts + mmff_vector, list query with rollup_points recursive CTE + 5→44 flow-state translation, 8 filter slots, 7-key sort whitelist, cross-DB owner decoration); Phase 3 wire GET /v1/api/v2/work-items with auth context (subscription_id from JWT, not query param), RequireAuth + RequireFreshPassword + LimitByIP, and the WORK_ITEMS_V2 env gate (503 when unset); Phase 4 ETL backfill via postgres_fdw from mmff_vector.obj_work_items into vector_artefacts.artefacts; Phase 5 parity smoke test, flag flip + frontend burn-in, finalisation docs and PLA close-out.
value: The legacy mmff_vector.obj_* work-items model conflates write-path mutation with read-path materialisation in one schema, blocks horizontal scaling because every tenant action shares one pgxpool, and forces every artefact-shaped resource (work-items, defects, sprints, events) to live as a sibling table inside the tenant DB. Moving artefacts into their own vector_artefacts database unlocks: (a) unified artefact storage in one wide artefacts table with type discrimination, ready for cross-tenant analytics that the per-tenant DB cannot serve; (b) independent connection pooling and tuning targets for the artefact workload (typically high-volume read with bursty writes) versus the tenant control-plane workload (low-volume but transactional); (c) a clean cutover surface for v2 endpoints — the WORK_ITEMS_V2 env gate lets per-environment opt-in without redeploy, and the parity smoke test gives a single command to assert that v1 and v2 return identical shapes for any client; (d) a place for future artefact subtypes (defects, sprints, retrospectives) to land without polluting the tenant schema. Five phases land zero-data-loss, gated by the env flag, with a per-phase rollback path: phase 1–3 are additive (new DB, new package, new route); phase 4 is idempotent ETL; phase 5 is the only step that takes user-visible reads off the v1 path.
implementation_plan: Phase 1 — Schema + fixture + register (00461–00463): migrations 012 (priority/story_points/due_date columns + indexes on artefacts), 013 (sprints table + FK artefacts.sprint_id), 014 (catch-up seed_system_artefact_types invocation for dev tenant), append self-invoke to 010 for fresh installs; seed/01_work_items_fixture.sql with 15 idempotent artefacts (3 epic / 6 story / 4 task / 2 defect); docs/c_schema.md vector_artefacts section + docs/c_c_v2_workitems_cutover_followups.md deferred-work register + .claude/handoffs/v2-cutover/ design artefacts.

Phase 2 — workitemsv2 Go package (00464–00468): Handler + Service + types mirroring v1 wire shape; second pgxpool for vector_artefacts (VECTOR_ARTEFACTS_DB_URL) with nil-tolerant fallback; ListWorkItems with rollup_points recursive CTE and flow_state_code translation (5-state internal vocabulary → 4-state external vocabulary); 8 filter slots (parent_id/item_type/status/priority/sprint_id/owner_id/limit/offset); ORDER BY whitelist (item_type/title/status/priority/points/sprint_id/due_date) with direction clamp; cross-DB owner decoration from mmff_vector.users via mainPool as a post-fetch batch; migrate runner gains a vector_artefacts case (VA_DB_* env vars).

Phase 3 — Auth wiring + env gate (00469, 00471): handler.go switches to auth.UserFromCtx for subscription_id (was a query param in stub); main.go promotes the route to r.Route with RequireAuth + RequireFreshPassword + LimitByIP(120/min); WORK_ITEMS_V2 env gate — 503 when unset, authenticated v2 route when "true".

Phase 4 — ETL backfill (00472): postgres_fdw foreign-server + foreign-table linking mmff_vector.obj_work_items as a remote source inside vector_artefacts; idempotent upsert into artefacts keyed on (subscription_id, source_id); column mapping covers item_type, flow_state, owner_id, story_points, due_date, sprint_id; restartable; produces a row-count audit table.

Phase 5 — Parity smoke test + flip + finalise (00473–00475): parity smoke test that hits /api/work-items and /api/v2/work-items with the same filter set + diff/asserts identical response shape; WORK_ITEMS_V2=true in dev .env, exercise the work-items page through the frontend until no regressions are observed; finalise documentation — backfill leaf doc closes, c_scope.md flips to v2, c_tech_debt.md records the v1 obj_work_items deprecation, PLA-0023 closes.
areas_impacted: Database (new): vector_artefacts — artefacts wide table + sprints + system artefact type seed + work_items fixture; migrations 010 (self-invoke append), 012 (artefact columns), 013 (sprints), 014 (seed catch-up).
Backend (Go, new): backend/internal/workitemsv2/ (Handler + Service + types); dual pgxpool with VECTOR_ARTEFACTS_DB_URL pool + nil-tolerant fallback to mmff_vector pool for owner decoration.

Backend (Go, modified): backend/cmd/server/main.go — NewService two-arg signature, GET /v1/api/v2/work-items stub then promotion to authenticated r.Route under WORK_ITEMS_V2 env gate; backend/cmd/migrate — vector_artefacts case + VA_DB_* env-var resolution.
Backend (Go, deferred phase 4): postgres_fdw foreign-server + foreign-table wiring inside vector_artefacts; idempotent ETL job that runs against mmff_vector.obj_work_items.

Frontend: WORK_ITEMS_V2 env gate flip in dev .env (phase 5); existing /work-items page exercised end-to-end against v2 — no code change expected if the wire shape parity smoke test passes (story 00473 is the gate).

Documentation: docs/c_schema.md (vector_artefacts section), docs/c_c_v2_workitems_cutover_followups.md (deferred-work register), docs/c_c_vector_artefacts_backfill.md (production cutover plan), docs/c_scope.md + docs/c_tech_debt.md (close-out updates in 00475).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Fixture is idempotent — re-running does not duplicate rows

ListWorkItems returns artefacts sorted by the requested column
rollup_points recursive CTE computes parent → child accumulation correctly
Internal 5-state flow vocabulary translates to the external 4-state vocabulary on output
All eight filter slots parse from query string into the handler
Unknown filter keys are rejected or silently ignored as designed
Filters compose into the dynamic WHERE clause without SQL injection risk
Seven ORDER BY columns are accepted and applied
Direction parameter is clamped to ASC/DESC
Unknown sort keys fall back to a stable default (item_type, title)
decorateOwners() batches mmff_vector.users lookups across artefact rows
Missing or unreachable owner records degrade to a placeholder
Owner decoration occurs after the artefacts query, not as a JOIN
handler.go derives subscription_id from auth.UserFromCtx, not the query string
Calls without an authenticated context return 401 (not 200 with empty data)
Tenant scoping is enforced in the SQL filter via auth-derived subscription_id
/v1/api/v2/work-items returns 503 when WORK_ITEMS_V2 env var is unset
With WORK_ITEMS_V2="true", the route is mounted under r.Route with RequireAuth + RequireFreshPassword + LimitByIP
Per-IP rate limit of 120/min applies to v2 work-items reads
vector_artefacts.artefacts row count after backfill equals obj_work_items row count for every subscription
Running the ETL a second time produces zero new rows (no duplicates on (subscription_id, source_legacy_id))
Sample row's flow_kind / parent_id / owner_id / priority / due_date match the source obj_work_items row exactly
Parity script exits 0 with no diffs across at least 12 filter/sort permutations
Any introduced wire-shape divergence (e.g. adding a field) trips the parity script with a structured diff
Output file dev/research/RNNN-pla0023-parity.json records the test matrix and each permutation's pass/fail
/work-items page renders v2 data with no console errors after the flag flip
Filter chips, sort headers, DnD reorder, bulk-action bar, and inline due-date editor each work against v2
A 30-minute burn-in produces no 5xx on /api/v2/work-items and no client-side error toasts
docs/c_c_vector_artefacts_backfill.md reflects as-built state with shipping commit hashes for 00461–00475
docs/c_plan_index.md shows PLA-0023 ... | complete | and dev/plans/PLA-0023.json.date_finished is set to today's date
Any deferred work has a row in docs/c_c_v2_workitems_cutover_followups.md with trigger + owner + status

risks.risk: Wire-shape divergence between v1 obj_work_items and v2 vector_artefacts.artefacts — a missed column mapping or flow-state translation gap silently breaks the work-items UI when WORK_ITEMS_V2=true
risks.mitigation: Story 00473 parity smoke test is a hard gate before story 00474 flag flip; the smoke test diffs every field of every row across the two endpoints with the same filter inputs and exits non-zero on mismatch.

risks.risk: ETL backfill via postgres_fdw is non-trivially correct — missing source rows, duplicate inserts on retry, or cross-DB transaction boundary surprises could produce a vector_artefacts state that disagrees with the live mmff_vector source
risks.mitigation: Story 00472 is idempotent (upsert keyed on (subscription_id, source_id)) and produces an audit table of source row counts vs target row counts; the smoke test in 00473 catches any residual divergence.

risks.risk: Two-pool service: a misconfigured VECTOR_ARTEFACTS_DB_URL can cause silent fallback to mmff_vector reads, masking a misrouted endpoint as "working" in dev
risks.mitigation: Service constructor logs which pool resolved to which DSN at startup; nil-pool branches return 503 explicitly rather than degrading silently; the env-badge degraded-state surface raises a panel-level warning when the v2 pool is missing.

risks.risk: WORK_ITEMS_V2 env gate could be left on in production after dev burn-in, exposing an only-partially-migrated read path
risks.mitigation: Story 00474 burn-in is bounded to the dev environment; production cutover is a separate decision recorded in docs/c_c_vector_artefacts_backfill.md and gated on a fresh parity smoke run against the production data.

risks.risk: Cross-DB owner decoration adds a per-request join window where mmff_vector.users could be unreachable while vector_artefacts is healthy — a transient mainPool outage would degrade the response
risks.mitigation: decorateOwners is best-effort: missing users degrade to a placeholder owner record rather than failing the request; the failure mode is logged at warn and surfaced on /api/status/pipeline.

references.label: docs/c_c_vector_artefacts_backfill.md — production cutover plan + ETL outline
docs/c_schema.md — schema overview (vector_artefacts section added in phase 1)

PostgreSQL — postgres_fdw (foreign-data wrapper used by phase 4 ETL)
pgx — pgxpool (per-database pools for two-pool service)
